channel.title: Regular Expressions on Jsj's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Regular Expressions on Jsj's Blog

item.title: regular expressions cheatsheet
item.description: Regular Expressions Matching Table metacharacter matching things \d \D matching all the digits(0 to 9), matching any non-digit character .(dot) matching any single character(letter, digit, whitespace, everything) [] matching a single letter inside [](square brackets) [^] matching any single character except for the letters inside [](square brackets) with the ^(hat) [-] [^-] indicating a character range to match a single character \w \W this is equivalent to the character range [A-Za-z0-9_], matching any non-alphanumeric character {} to match repetition of character * + to match either 0 or more or 1 or more of the character that it follows(it always follows a character or group) ?
